subject: Mathematics
title: Tangent Lines
description: A micro-lesson that makes Tangent Lines usable.

hook.text: A tangent line touches a circle at exactly one point and then continues straight.

buildup.text: At the point of contact, the tangent is always perpendicular to the radius.

discovery.text: This property lets engineers calculate gear teeth, roller coasters, and road curves.

twist.text: Tangents also appear in calculus. The tangent to any curve shows its instantaneous direction.

climax.text: From circles to curves to derivatives, the tangent is one of geometry's most versatile tools.

punchline.text: One point of contact reveals the curve's direction.

quiz.question: At the point of tangency, how is the tangent related to the radius?
